step_by_step_thinking:
Acid etching primarily serves to enhance the adhesion of subsequent layers by creating a roughened surface. Among the given options:
\boxed{A} Decreasing micro-leakage would be one beneficial outcome, but it’s more directly related to improving seal quality rather than being the "most advantageous" reason for acid etching itself.
\boxed{A}
This indicates that although reducing microleakage is important, the direct advantage of acid etching pertains more fundamentally to enhancing the micromechanical bond strength and overall performance of restoratives, thus aligning best with option A. Acid etching prepares the substrate for optimal adhesion and integration, thereby contributing indirectly but importantly to minimizing microleakage. Therefore, focusing on the fundamental benefit provides clearer support for choosing
